figures.caption: Figure 6.2: Twin-plant view of non-diagnosability: after the first error on the upper run, the lower run stays fault-free yet observationally indistinguishable for the whole time window of length D.
Figure 3.1: Decentralized diagnosis via local knowledge monitors (positive case). Each global event σ is locally filtered into obsai(σ) and processed by the corresponding monitor.

Figure 6.4: Shape of the reduction for monitorability of timed-automaton languages. After the marker #, the construction exposes whether the simulated run of A was in an accepting state. In the lower branch, the additional rightmost state ensures that no finite continuation yields a definitive verdict.
Figure 2.1: Belief update and representative state transition.
Figure 6.3: Three-valued timed monitor for Gψ. Mode ? means “true so far, but not yet guaranteed forever”. Mode ⊤ means “permanently safe”, and mode ⊥ means “already violated”.
